ddls:
CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the provider of the event */,
  "event_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the event level (e.g., Error, Information, Warning) */,
  "event_id_num" INTEGER /* Numeric identifier of the event */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the event */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the channel where the event is logged */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the task associated with the event */,
  "opcode_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the opcode of the event */,
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the keyword of the event */,
  "message" TEXT /* Detailed message of the event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event occurred */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event (e.g., Critical, High, Medium, Low) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the event (e.g., Performance, Security, Capacity) */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the event occurred */,
  "storage_pool_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the storage pool associated with the event */,
  "physical_disk_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the physical disk associated with the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_event_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_level_id") REFERENCES event_levels ("event_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_keyword_id FOREIGN KEY ("keyword_id") REFERENCES keywords ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_storage_pool_id FOREIGN KEY ("storage_pool_id") REFERENCES storage_pools ("pool_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_provider_id FOREIGN KEY ("provider_id") REFERENCES providers ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_physical_disk_id FOREIGN KEY ("physical_disk_id") REFERENCES physical_disks ("disk_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_opcode_id FOREIGN KEY ("opcode_id") REFERENCES opcodes ("opcode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the provider */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the provider */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the provider (e.g., email, phone number) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_levels" (
  "event_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event level */,
  "event_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the event level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event level */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event level (e.g., Critical, High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of channel (e.g., Operational, Administrative) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the task */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the task (e.g., Maintenance, Troubleshooting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "opcodes" (
  "opcode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each opcode */,
  "opcode_name" TEXT /* Name of the opcode */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the opcode */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the opcode (e.g., System, Application) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("opcode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword_name" TEXT /* Name of the keyword */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the keyword */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the keyword (e.g., Performance, Security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "storage_pools" (
  "pool_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storage pool */,
  "pool_name" TEXT /* Name of the storage pool */,
  "total_capacity" TEXT /* Total capacity of the storage pool in bytes */,
  "used_capacity" TEXT /* Used capacity of the storage pool in bytes */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the storage pool (e.g., Online, Failed, Degraded) */,
  "threshold_limit" TEXT /* Threshold limit for capacity consumption in bytes */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the pool was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the pool was last updated */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the pool is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pool_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_storage_pools_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "physical_disks" (
  "disk_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each physical disk */,
  "disk_name" TEXT /* Name of the physical disk */,
  "pool_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the storage pool the disk belongs to */,
  "total_capacity" TEXT /* Total capacity of the physical disk in bytes */,
  "used_capacity" TEXT /* Used capacity of the physical disk in bytes */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the physical disk (e.g., Online, Failed, Offline) */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* Health status of the physical disk (e.g., Healthy, Unhealthy, Warning) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the disk was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the disk was last updated */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the disk */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the disk */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disk_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_physical_disks_pool_id FOREIGN KEY ("pool_id") REFERENCES storage_pools ("pool_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, engineer, auditor) */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the user belongs to */,
  "job_function" TEXT /* Job function of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "data_center_name" TEXT /* Name of the data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the data center */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event that triggered the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)
